# Tending

> Tending (tending.network) is a relationship management tool for social
> purpose organisations — charities, community groups, mutual aid networks,
> social enterprises. It is a relationship-first rethink of a CRM:
> relationships are living narratives, not database records.

## What it does

- **Moments**: users write short natural-language notes ("Bumped into Amara
  at the allotment — she offered to introduce me to the council food team").
  Tending recognises the people, organisations and spaces mentioned and links
  them automatically. Moments can also be spoken aloud (voice transcription).
- **The living network**: a breathing, force-directed map of every
  relationship, with vitality encoding — fresh relationships glow, quiet
  ones fade — and constellation clustering of groups that appear together.
- **Stories**: an AI-written narrative for each relationship, updated as
  moments accrue.
- **Field notes (observations)**: pattern detection that gently surfaces
  dormant relationships, quality shifts, and dependency risks (e.g. "all
  your funder relationships flow through one person").
- **Spaces**: the real-world places and gatherings where relationships
  happen — allotments, assemblies, church halls.

## Pricing

Flat £5/month (GBP), everything included, no tiers. 30-day free trial of
all features, no card required.

## Audience

Social purpose organisations in the UK and beyond: charities, community
organisers, funders, mutual aid groups — anyone whose work runs on
relationships rather than sales pipelines.

## Key pages

- https://tending.network/ — product overview
- https://tending.network/pricing — pricing
- https://tending.network/privacy — privacy policy
- https://tending.network/terms — terms of service
